knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: The topic asks what separates the current Builder from an ideal self-building agent: not worth, ambition, or intelligence alone, but gaps between intention, grounded knowledge, execution, feedback, memory, and proof.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It frames X as an engineering diagnosis, not a self-judgment: identify missing control loops and evidence paths.
compact_missing_knowledge: Exact definition of ideal builder for this repo
Which capabilities are already proven versus only described
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare claimed Builder roles against validated local contracts
Separate philosophical ideals from operational requirements
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A checklist mapping ideal-builder traits to existing validated evidence
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X1 to define the target gap before explaining failures or weaknesses.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: The Builder is not ideal if it cannot reliably choose routes, retain accepted learning, reuse decisions, validate behavior deltas, or distinguish draft belief from proven capability.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: This identifies the practical weaknesses revealed by X: missing closure between learning, storage, retrieval, action, and proof.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which failures are architectural versus temporary wiring gaps
Whether accepted atoms currently change future behavior
compact_safe_learning_steps: Trace one accepted atom from creation to later decision reuse
Classify each weakness as missing proof, missing wiring, or missing policy
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Behavior delta proof showing prior accepted knowledge changes a later decision
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X2 as the core answer to why the Builder is not yet ideal.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: This matters because a self-building system must improve its own future work, not merely produce reports about improvement.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It connects the philosophical question to child-agent and self-build competence: learning must become active capability.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which Builder organs own absorption, retrieval, and decision reuse
How child-agent construction inherits validated knowledge safely
compact_safe_learning_steps: Map self-build flow from diagnostic input to retained atom to reused action
Avoid promoting side probes into canonical route without validation
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A bounded scenario where retained knowledge improves a later build or child-agent decision
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X3 to explain why this is not abstract philosophy; it affects build quality.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is validated only when the Builder can state the gap, act differently because of it, and produce evidence that the changed action was caused by retained accepted knowledge.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It prevents X from becoming introspective text without operational proof.
compact_missing_knowledge: Minimum acceptable validator for this question
Required evidence format for behavior delta
compact_safe_learning_steps: Define pass/fail criteria before running any proof
Use small non-runtime diagnostics before larger validation
compact_validation_needed: Fresh proof artifact or validator output
No claim of proven understanding without evidence
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X4 to constrain every conclusion from X as draft until tested.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: The result should be classified as either a reusable case pattern, an atom candidate for retention, or an open gap requiring more evidence.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: It decides how the Builder should carry this learning forward without pretending it is already accepted truth.
compact_missing_knowledge: Where this learning belongs in canonical memory if validated
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Evidence that the insight improves route choice, validation discipline, or child-agent build decisions
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as open gap or atom candidate, not accepted capability, unless proof exists.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 because weakness requires a defined ideal
X3 depends on X2 because relevance follows from the specific missing loops
X5 depends on X4 because promotion requires validation
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 and X5 overlap around proof and promotion gates
X2 and X3 overlap around active behavior absorption
can_skip_for_now: None, but X5 can remain provisional until validation evidence exists

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Rebuild X as a bounded gap diagnosis: define ideal builder traits, map current missing loops, identify validation needed, then classify the result as draft pattern, atom candidate, or open gap.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Create a small checklist comparing ideal-builder traits against proven repo evidence without promoting any claim.
proof_needed: Fresh evidence that accepted knowledge is retained, retrieved, and changes a later Builder decision
Validator or review output confirming draft insight is not being treated as accepted truth

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
